description: DisplayedSkills is declared as 'private static readonly SkillType[]' (a plain array). Arrays are mutable in C# — any code can write to elements even through a readonly reference. While this specific array is only ever iterated and never mutated after the static initializer, the type gives no compile-time guarantee of immutability. If any future code accidentally assigns to an index (e.g. DisplayedSkills[0] = ...) it would silently affect all concurrent renders sharing this static field.
suggestedFix: Change the type to IReadOnlyList<SkillType> to enforce immutability at the type level: 'private static readonly IReadOnlyList<SkillType> DisplayedSkills = [SkillType.Keeper, SkillType.Defending, SkillType.Playmaking, SkillType.Winger, SkillType.Scoring, SkillType.Passing, SkillType.SetPieces];'
